popularStory.headline: Adam Mott
popularStory.description:
Adam Mott was married first to Elizabeth Creel and his second wife was Sarah Lott.  Sarah maiden name was Jennings and she married her first husband Englebert Lott.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Creel於1600年出生在英國坎布利其爾坎布利其時，她的父親John Thomas Creel III25歲，母親Nancy J. Stampe21歲。她於1616年10月28日與Adam Mott在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1632年在她的家鄉享年32歲，埋葬在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Creel于1600年出生在英国坎布利其爾坎布利其时，她的父亲John Thomas Creel III25岁，母亲Nancy J. Stampe21岁。她于1616年10月28日与Adam Mott在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1632年在她的家乡享年32岁，埋葬在英国艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

nameMeanings.name: Creel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Possibly an Americanized form of German Kriel or Krüll, a variant of Kroll 1.

variants.name: Kriel
meanings: German: habitational name from a place called Kriele, near Rathenow (Brandenburg). This surname is rare in Germany.
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Croyle  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Creel was born in 1600, in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, her father, John Thomas Creel III, was 25 and her mother, Nancy J. Stampe, was 21. She married Adam Mott on 28 October 1616, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1632, in her hometown, at the age of 32, and was buried in Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Creel est née en 1600, à Canteburge, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, John Thomas Creel III, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Nancy J. Stampe, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Adam Mott le 28 octobre 1616, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1632, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre.

de.Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Coloni: Portsmouth, Rhode Island, British Colonial America
de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Creel 1600 in Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Thomas Creel III 25 und ihre Mutter Nancy J. Stampe 21. Sie hat am 28. Oktober 1616 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England Adam Mott geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1632 im Alter von 32 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Saffron Walden, Essex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Creel è nata il nel 1600 nel Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, England, sua padre, John Thomas Creel III, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Nancy J. Stampe, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Adam Mott il 28 ottobre 1616, nel Saffron Walden, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1632, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolta a Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Creel(이)가 1600년 영국 캄브리지실르 캄브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Thomas Creel III(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Nancy J. Stampe(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1616년 10월 28일 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 Adam Mott(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1632년 그녀의 고향에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Creel nasceu em 1600, em Cambridge, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Thomas Creel III, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Nancy J. Stampe, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Adam Mott em 28 de outubro de 1616, em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1632, em sua cidade natal, com 32 anos, e foi sepultada em Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Creel родилась в 1600 г. в местности камбридж, камбриджешир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Thomas Creel III было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Nancy J. Stampe, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Adam Mott 28 октября 1616 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1632 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия.

Se casó con Adam Mott el 28 de octubre de 1616, en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1632, en su pueblo, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultada en Saffron Walden, Essex, Inglaterra.
